summary: Provision IAM Access Analyzer. Creating an analyzer is an engineer-gated form; an organization-scoped analyzer reads across every account, so its scope is reviewed, never blind self-service.

operations.title: Provision an access analyzer
operations.summary: Create an IAM Access Analyzer. An organization-scoped analyzer reads across every account in the org.
operations.description: Create an aws_accessanalyzer_analyzer that reports resources shared outside a zone of trust. The analyzer type (account vs organization, access vs unused-access) sets how far it reads, so it is bounded to an allowlist. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: analyzer_name
params.label: Analyzer name
params.help: The name of the analyzer (written to the analyzer_name attribute).

params.name: analyzer_type
params.label: Analyzer type
params.help: ACCOUNT analyzes this account; ORGANIZATION analyzes the whole org; the UNUSED_ACCESS variants find unused permissions.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: An organization-scoped analyzer reads across every account in the org; confirm the scope (account vs organization) matches the reviewing team's authority.
